title: Jest vs Integration Script Testing Approach
description: Clarify testing strategy: Jest for unit tests, integration scripts for E2E, Flutter tests for UI. Clear documentation on when to use which framework.
blocking_items: CI/CD unsure which framework to run
Difficult to achieve >80% code coverage target

correction_steps.details: Establish coverage: 50% unit tests (Jest), 30% integration tests (scripts), 20% E2E tests (Selenium/Appium for Flutter). Unit tests: route-level validation. Integration tests: full workflows. E2E tests: user scenarios

Expand backend/__tests__ with focused test files: auth.unit.test.js, translations.unit.test.js, middleware.unit.test.js, utils.unit.test.js. Mock dependencies (database, external APIs). Run offline, fast (<1s)

correction_steps.task: Refactor Integration Scripts as Jest Suites
correction_steps.details: Convert backend/scripts/test_sprint*.js patterns into Jest test suites. Keep live API testing as separate 'integration' test type in Jest config. Maintain current integration scripts as manual E2E validation tools
Add mobile/test/ with: api_service_test.dart (mock HTTP), ml_service_test.dart (mock model), ui_widget_test.dart (widget rendering). Use mockito for mocking. Coverage target: >70%
Add mobile/integration_test/ with full app flows: auth_flow_test.dart, translation_flow_test.dart. Use integration_test package. Run against real backend. Coverage: main user journeys

Add ml/tests/ with: test_model_loading.py (verify TensorFlow Lite loads), test_inference.py (verify predictions return correct shape), test_accuracy.py (benchmark inference speed). Use pytest framework
Create TESTING.md: 'npm test' (Jest unit+integration), 'npm run test:e2e' (live API), 'flutter test', 'pytest'. Include expected runtimes, parallelization strategy, debugging tips
Add coverage configuration: Jest generates coverage.json; combine with flutter coverage; create merged report. Use nyc or similar tool to aggregate. Minimum: >80% for production code

validation: Jest tests run in <30 seconds; Flutter tests run in <60 seconds; coverage reports generated with >80% for core modules; CI can run all tests automatically
error_handling: If coverage low: identify untested functions, add unit tests. If tests flaky: mock external dependencies, add retry logic, use test data factory

title: UI and Accessibility Testing
description: No accessibility or UI compliance testing. Need to add Flutter widget tests and accessibility validation to ensure app is usable by all users.
blocking_items: No verification that UI is accessible to users with disabilities

correction_steps.task: Create Widget Tests for All Screens
correction_steps.details: Create mobile/test/widgets/ with tests for: LoginScreen, RegisterScreen, TranslationScreen, SettingsScreen. Verify widgets render, buttons clickable, text visible. Use WidgetTester framework
Use flutter test with Semantics layer verification: all buttons have labels, images have descriptions, text contrast >4.5:1. Check: key navigation, screen reader support, semantic tree correctness
Create mobile/test/responsive_test.dart: test layouts on phone (360x640), tablet (600x800), landscape (800x360). Verify text readable, buttons reachable, layout doesn't break
If app supports multiple languages: create mobile/test/localization_test.dart. Verify all strings translated, text rendering correct for each locale, RTL support (if applicable)
Create mobile/test/theme_test.dart: verify colors accessible (pass WCAG AAA), text sizing consistent, typography hierarchy clear. Test light and dark modes if supported
Test all user inputs: email validation, password complexity display, form error messages. Verify invalid inputs rejected with clear messaging. Test XSS prevention
Create mobile/test/performance_test.dart: measure UI render time (<16ms for 60fps), measure model inference on real device, measure API response time. Log baseline metrics

validation: All widgets render correctly; accessibility tests pass (WCAG 2.1 AA minimum); responsive design verified on multiple screen sizes; performance benchmarks established
error_handling: If accessibility tests fail: add semantic labels, verify text contrast with tools like WebAIM. If performance slow: profile with Flutter DevTools, optimize widget rebuilds, lazy load data

title: Integration Test Coverage for All API Endpoints
description: Need comprehensive end-to-end test coverage for all API endpoints and workflows to ensure system stability before Phase 6.
blocking_items: Incomplete test coverage for edge cases

correction_steps.details: Create backend/__tests__/integration.test.js with test cases for each endpoint: 1) POST /auth/register: valid, invalid email, weak password, duplicate email. 2) POST /auth/login: valid, wrong password, nonexistent user. 3) POST /translations: valid, invalid confidence, no auth. 4) GET /translations: paginated results, user isolation, sorting

Create exhaustive error scenario tests: 400 (validation errors), 401 (auth errors), 409 (duplicate), 500 (server errors). Verify error response format, status codes, error codes. Test timeout handling
Create test with multiple users: User A creates translation, User B tries to access User A's translation. Verify User B gets empty list or 403. Test pagination, filtering per user

Create 100 test translations for one user. Test: 1) GET /translations?page=1&limit=10, verify returns 10 items. 2) GET /translations?page=2&limit=10, verify different items. 3) GET /translations?page=100, verify empty if out of range. Test default pagination
Create test with JWTs: issue short-expiry token (5 seconds), wait 6 seconds, try API call, verify 401. Test token refresh (if implemented), verify new token works
Create test hitting rate limits: 1) Send 6 auth requests in 15 seconds, verify 5th succeeds, 6th returns 429. 2) Repeat for register (10/hour). 3) Test reset after time window
For each endpoint, test: 1) Missing required fields. 2) Invalid field types. 3) Out-of-range values (confidence >1). 4) XSS payloads (escaped correctly). 5) SQL injection attempts (no vulnerability)
Create multiple users, make concurrent requests: multiple registrations, simultaneous translations, concurrent reads. Verify no data corruption, no race conditions
Single test covering full flow: 1) Register user. 2) Login. 3) Create translation. 4) Get translations. 5) Verify translation in list. 6) Logout. Should complete in <5 seconds

validation: >95% endpoint coverage with tests; all error scenarios tested; concurrent operations safe; user isolation verified; pagination working correctly
error_handling: If tests fail: check backend logs, verify test data setup, check database state before/after each test. Use test fixtures for consistent setup

title: Performance Benchmarking and Load Testing
description: No performance baselines or load testing. Need to establish performance targets and verify system can handle realistic load.
blocking_items: Unknown ML inference speed on device

correction_steps.details: Create backend/scripts/perf_test.js: measure response time for each endpoint: auth/register (<200ms), auth/login (<200ms), POST translation (<300ms), GET translations (<500ms for 10 items). Run 100x, calculate p50, p95, p99 latencies

Create backend/scripts/db_perf_test.js: measure query times with different datasets: 0 translations (baseline), 100, 1000, 10000. Track: user find (<10ms), translation find (<50ms), pagination (<100ms). Identify slow queries

Use Apache JMeter or k6 tool. Create load test: 1) Simulate 10 concurrent users for 1 minute. 2) Gradually increase to 100 users. 3) Measure: response time distribution, error rate, throughput (requests/sec). Identify breaking point
Create mobile/test/ml_performance_test.dart: measure inference time on real device (not emulator). Baseline target: <200ms per frame. Measure memory usage. Test with 30fps camera stream
Measure total latency from camera frame → ML inference → API POST → response: target <1 second. Break down: capture (16ms), inference (200ms), API (300ms), response (100ms)

Create PERFORMANCE.md: 1) API targets: register/login <200ms, POST <300ms, GET <500ms. 2) DB targets: query <50ms. 3) ML target: <200ms inference. 4) E2E target: <1s camera→result. Measured on reference hardware
Add performance test to CI/CD: run on every commit, compare against baseline. Flag PRs that degrade performance >10%. Archive historical metrics

validation: Performance baselines established and documented; load test shows system stable up to target concurrent users; no regressions detected in CI
error_handling: If performance degrades: profile with DevTools, identify bottleneck (DB query, API processing, frontend rendering), optimize. If load test fails: scale backend (more workers), add caching, optimize queries
